# Workspace Instructions

This repository is a simple, static personal profile website hosted with GitHub Pages.

## What this project is

- Pure HTML and CSS only.
- No build step is required.
- Pages are served from `index.html`, `work/index.html`, `opensource/index.html`, and `writing/index.html`.
- Shared styling lives in `assets/css/style.css`.

## Use this guidance when editing

- Keep changes small, semantic, and consistent with the existing site structure.
- Follow the current design patterns used in the site: simple HTML sections, responsive layout, and minimal inline styling.
- Prefer edits to existing pages and reuse existing classes before adding new custom styles.
- Do not introduce JavaScript frameworks, build tooling, or backend/server code.

## Content rules

- Only include public, professional information.
- Do not add personal contact details such as private email addresses, phone numbers, home addresses, or confidential data.
- Public professional profiles like GitHub and LinkedIn are acceptable.
- Do not expose credentials, API keys, environment variables, or secrets.

## Site-specific notes

- Maintain the navigation structure and keep the site easy to scan.
- New pages must be linked from the nav and match the same layout style.
- Avoid external tracking scripts or third-party analytics.
- Keep the content consistent with a portfolio/profile website, focused on work, open source, writing, and professional skills.

## Development

- Open the HTML files directly in a browser or use a live preview extension.
- Editing should be limited to the repository contents; no external deployment config is required beyond GitHub Pages.
